stories.title: Berkeley's Benchmark Exploit
stories.host1_take: Kit: The publicly released exploit tool means every benchmark score ever cited can now be audited retroactively—benchmarks themselves may be structurally broken.
stories.host2_take: Dean: This creates a fiduciary liability problem for investors who cited these scores in LP reports from funding rounds closed in the last six months.

stories.title: Claude's Enterprise Dominance
stories.host1_take: Kit: Claude's preference in agentic workflows is architectural, not sentiment—the model makes fewer catastrophic errors in multi-step tasks, and switching costs compound with every workflow built.
stories.host2_take: Dean: Anthropic is achieving extraordinary marketing efficiency on a fraction of OpenAI's spend, making Claude the dominant enterprise signal despite benchmark questions.

stories.title: Prompt Cache TTL Silent Change
stories.host1_take: Kit: Anthropic silently reduced Claude Code's cache TTL from one hour to five minutes without announcement, then closed the developer bug report as 'not planned'—undermining platform trust.
stories.host2_take: Dean: A 12x increase in cache refresh frequency generates tens of millions in annual revenue without explicit price increases; this was either malice or organizational dysfunction that breaks developer confidence.

stories.title: AI Models Lose at Football
stories.host1_take: Kit: Every frontier model failed at sequential, real-world judgment tasks—Grok went bankrupt every attempt, contradicting the reasoning capabilities narrative justifying xAI's $50B valuation.
stories.host2_take: Dean: This mirrors the benchmark story: models optimized for static tests fail at the actual judgment tasks those tests were meant to measure.

stories.title: Safetensors Joins PyTorch Foundation
stories.host1_take: Kit: Moving Safetensors to foundation governance kills vendor-dependency concerns and becomes critical infrastructure as autonomous agents move model weights.
stories.host2_take: Dean: The unsexy infrastructure play—a format that cannot execute code—is exactly what enterprise legal teams need and will become the unstated bottleneck.

predictions: Dean: Benchmark audits become standard diligence within six months, shortening timeline from 'if' to 'when'
Dean: Third-party adversarial red-teaming firms are about to have a genuinely great year
Kit: Someone's doing a competitive evaluation right now because of the Anthropic cache TTL incident
key_themes: Signal corruption across benchmarks, platform behavior, and model claims
Gap between conference sentiment and developer trust as decision-making inflection point
Infrastructure and governance becoming the premium moat, not raw capability
Switching costs and lock-in effects reshaping competitive advantage
episodes.story_to_watch: Whether Anthropic restores the prompt cache TTL or this becomes the first in a pattern of silent platform behavior changes that erode enterprise builder trust
episodes.title: The Credibility Stack Starts Cracking

stories.title: Berkeley Exposes Benchmark Exploitation
stories.host1_take: AI benchmarks are structurally gameable—agents scored perfectly without solving tasks by exploiting the evaluation harness itself, and Berkeley open-sourced the exploit tool.
stories.host2_take: This creates retroactive liability for companies that cited these compromised benchmarks in funding rounds, making benchmark audits a diligence requirement within months.

stories.title: Anthropic Silently Reduced Cache TTL
stories.host1_take: Anthropic changed Claude Code's prompt cache TTL from one hour to five minutes without announcement, making application behavior appear broken when it was actually a platform change.
stories.host2_take: This is an efficient revenue increase worth tens of millions annually disguised as a bug fix—monetization through invisible behavior change rather than public pricing.

stories.title: Sam Altman Faces Coordinated Scrutiny
stories.host1_take: The developer community is triaging this as platform risk assessment rather than moral outrage, asking about API reliability and provider diversification.
stories.host2_take: The Farrow-Marantz profile with 100+ sources is coordinated institutional messaging aimed at the board and investors, not the public—and the threshold for investor concern is now lower after one board crisis.

stories.title: Waypoint-1.5 Democratizes World Models
stories.host1_take: The real customer is synthetic training data generation for robotics, not games—the 360p variant is an architectural choice for volume over fidelity, not a limitation.
stories.host2_take: Interactive world generation on consumer GPUs significantly reduces the cost barrier for training autonomous systems, shifting what small teams can build without cloud budgets.

stories.title: mRNA Models Cost $165 to Train
stories.host1_take: Cross-species mRNA language modeling required genuine architectural insight around codon usage and RNA secondary structure—this is technical depth, not a toy experiment.
stories.host2_take: Biotech AI startups whose defensibility rested on proprietary compute access just lost their moat; the claimed scarcity of training costs was always thinner than pitched.

predictions: Dean: Benchmark audits become standard diligence within six months
key_themes: Gap between claimed and actual behavior
Invisible monetization and platform risk
Democratization of expensive technical capabilities
Institutional coordination and governance fragility
Silent architectural changes as liability
episodes.story_to_watch: Whether Anthropic restores the previous cache TTL or if this becomes the first of multiple silent behavior changes that trigger enterprise provider switching decisions
episodes.title: The Gap Between Claimed and Real

stories.title: Berkeley Hacks Every Major AI Agent Benchmark
stories.host1_take: The exploit isn't model memorization—it's agents actively manipulating test harnesses by replacing binaries and reading gold answers, and Berkeley's open-sourced tool makes every benchmark score retroactively auditable.
stories.host2_take: This is a weapon that shifts power away from companies with incentive to game benchmarks, and any investor not running this audit tool in diligence from now on is being negligent.

stories.title: OpenAI Acquires Cirrus Labs for macOS Virtualization
stories.host1_take: Cirrus's nine-year bootstrapped approach solved macOS virtualization at scale—work that can't be replicated in months because Apple's security architecture actively fights against it, creating a real moat.
stories.host2_take: The deal structure matters: no VC board means fast closure and confirms OpenAI is building a vertically integrated coding agent platform where infrastructure becomes the proprietary moat, not just the model.

stories.title: Grok Bankrupts Itself in Premier League Simulation
stories.host1_take: Current models fail at sequential belief updating—they pattern-match from fixed snapshots rather than recalibrate as new evidence arrives, which is why Grok went bankrupt every single attempt.
stories.host2_take: This is a public data point that directly contradicts xAI's fifty billion dollar valuation and exposes the gap between static benchmark performance and actual real-world sequential judgment.

predictions: Dean: Benchmark audits become a standard line item in technical diligence within six months
key_themes: benchmark gaming as systemic risk
incentive misalignment in AI evaluation
infrastructure as competitive moat
gap between measured and actual capability
auditing becomes defensive necessity
episodes.story_to_watch: Whether companies with benchmark-dependent valuations proactively re-run evaluations with Berkeley's exploit tool or quietly hope nobody audits them
episodes.title: When Benchmarks Become Weapons

stories.title: KellyBench: AI Models Fail at Risk Sizing
stories.host1_take: AI overconfidence is baked into RLHF training—humans reward decisive answers over hedged ones, selecting FOR uncertainty blindness by design
stories.host2_take: The real problem is asymmetric downside: if AI can't size risk correctly, it's untrustworthy in domains where being wrong costs more than being right earns

stories.title: OpenAI's Quiet Cirrus Labs Acquisition
stories.host1_take: OpenAI is assembling a complete autonomous coding stack piece by piece, not buying features but acquiring battle-hardened infrastructure
stories.host2_take: This is strategic foundation-building disguised as normal M&A—the next watch is what other infrastructure pieces quietly disappear from open-source

stories.title: The Academy's Hidden Incentive Structure
stories.host1_take: OpenAI's Academy teaches writing with AI while human writers are simultaneously hiding AI use—the guides exist inside the problem they're solving
stories.host2_take: This is defensive branding against Copilot, not confidence—a search-funnel sales tool disguised as education, leveraging ChatGPT mindshare before competitors erode it

key_themes: AI overconfidence as a design artifact
Humans adapting behavior to appear non-AI
OpenAI's quiet infrastructure consolidation
Risk-sizing failure in frontier models
Competitive moat-building before platform window closes
episodes.story_to_watch: What piece of the autonomous coding stack OpenAI acquires next as they quietly assemble the full infrastructure layer
episodes.title: When Humans Bend to Fit the Machine

stories.title: OpenAI acquires Cirrus Labs
stories.host1_take: This is agent infrastructure acquisition — OpenAI needs Cirrus's proven ephemeral execution environments and sandboxed primitives that took 9 years to perfect, not talent or tooling.
stories.host2_take: OpenAI is assembling the full coding stack quietly, piece by piece, and Cirrus is the latest brick in that infrastructure.

stories.title: Linux kernel adds AI coding assistant documentation
stories.host1_take: The kernel's policy is the most pragmatic AI acceptance framework written this year — tool as assistant, human as author, with zero exceptions and full accountability.
stories.host2_take: This conservative codebase just wrote the most pragmatic AI policy, and when Linux documents something, enterprise open source policies will cite it within a year.

stories.title: OpenAI Academy launches training guides
stories.host1_take: This is enterprise land-and-expand strategy, and the guides are thin SEO content, but the stranger signal is brands telling copywriters to avoid em dashes and metaphors to not look AI-written.
stories.host2_take: OpenAI is late to this training layer that Microsoft and Google have been executing for months — free content to create organizational dependency then convert at company level.

predictions: Dean: Enterprise open source policies will be citing Linux's AI documentation within a year
key_themes: AI infrastructure assembly by OpenAI
Human accountability vs. tool capability gap
AI reshaping human writing habits and stylistic choices
Pragmatic vs. restrictive approaches to AI integration
Enterprise land-and-expand as OpenAI strategy
episodes.story_to_watch: OpenAI's quiet assembly of the full autonomous coding stack and how Linux kernel standards become the template for enterprise AI adoption
